name: api-design
tagline: REST API design reference covering resource naming, status codes, pagination, filtering, auth, rate limiting, and versioning with.
verdict: Thorough, internally consistent reference doc with strong worked examples across TypeScript, Python, and Go, and clear decision tables for pagination/versioning choices. No safety concerns — pure documentation, no commands executed. Weak spots: description doesn't state trigger phrasing, no scope-exclusion guidance, three full per-language code blocks sit.

highlights.summary: Description lists topics covered ('resource naming, status codes, pagination...') but never states a usage situation or trigger phrase.
No 'NOT for' or out-of-scope section anywhere in description or body.

Full TypeScript, Python, and Go implementation examples are pasted inline (~lines 400-505) though only one language is ever relevant per task — an obvious split point into per-language reference files
